basics.name: Pranesh Mukhopadhyay
basics.summary: Backend and applied ML engineer with 5.5+ years of experience designing and deploying production-grade Python systems. Specialized in FastAPI-based microservices, real-time decision systems, and ML-powered APIs in high-throughput environments.

projects.name: Real-time Order Assignment & Dispatch System
highlights: Designed asynchronous real-time order assignment system for dynamic batching and delivery partner allocation
Handled 30+ orders per minute per instance with real-time agent discovery
Built Celery + Redis workflows with centralized logging and production-grade reliability

projects.name: Order ETA Prediction Service
highlights: Built stateless ML service for real-time delivery ETA prediction
Designed feature pipelines using historical and real-time signals
Improved ETA accuracy by 75% using geofence-based caching

projects.name: Agent-based SKU Onboarding & Validation Pipeline
highlights: Built LLM-based agent pipeline for SKU onboarding and validation
Added observability across validation stages
Reduced manual effort by 16–20 hours/week

projects.name: Platform Reliability & Engineering Practices
highlights: Introduced centralized logging and structured error reporting
Achieved 80–90%+ test coverage using Pytest
Performed load testing using Locust

projects.name: Spiritzone Product Recommendation System
highlights: Built hybrid recommendation system using user behavior
Implemented incremental training pipelines
Exposed recommendations via stateless services

projects.name: Market Basket Analysis Service
highlights: Developed frequent item association service
Automated daily retraining and deployment
Achieved 90%+ test coverage

projects.name: Clickstream Data Analytics Pipeline
highlights: Built scalable ingestion pipeline for event data
Implemented scheduling and monitoring
Handled variable data rates without data loss

projects.name: Monolg
highlights: Plug-and-play structured logging library
Published on PyPI with CI-backed testing
Documented on ReadTheDocs

projects.name: YouTube Subtitle Dataset
projects.description: Dataset of 2,500+ YouTube subtitles with annotations
highlights: Curated dataset for NLP and classification use cases
Published on Kaggle
